title: A Look at the Tools Being Deployed to Help China’s Banks Dispose of Their NPLs
dek: An overview of legal, regulatory, and market mechanisms banks use to transfer or resolve bad assets.

sections.heading: Issue overview
paragraphs: This issue examines the tools being deployed to help China’s banks dispose of nonperforming loans, focusing on policy infrastructure, disposal channels, and the growing role of specialized credit investors.

China’s banking system continues to rely on asset-management companies, loan transfers, restructurings, and other workout channels to move problem assets off balance sheets and into the hands of investors equipped to resolve them.

For private credit investors, the issue highlights how disposal mechanisms can create opportunities where borrowers, collateral, and creditors require practical restructuring capital and local workout expertise.
